summary.intent: Persist open tabs across app restarts so users see the same tabs when they reopen the Electron app.
summary.outcome: Tab state persistence was implemented using localStorage in the renderer, with spawn tabs excluded on restore (their PTY is gone) and dedicated windows unaffected.

repo: Tab state lives entirely in TabsContext.tsx — the single source of truth for tab management in the renderer.
Dedicated terminal windows use a ?tab= URL param to identify their tab; the main window does not, enabling selective persistence logic.
Spawn tabs (SpawnSessionScreen) have ephemeral PTY state that cannot be restored after app restart.
SessionDetail.tsx accepts a hideTerminal flag via location.state to prevent double-rendering the terminal when spawned from SpawnSessionScreen.
Live sessions use a 500ms polling loop in SessionDetail.tsx rather than relying solely on the server's 2s broadcast interval.
workflow: localStorage is sufficient for tab persistence in Electron — no need to involve the main process or preload scripts.
Resuming a session starts by reading modified files and git diff to reconstruct context before proceeding.

friction: Session had to be resumed from scratch, requiring multiple file reads and a git diff to reconstruct prior context before proceeding.
open_items: SpawnSessionScreen.tsx was untracked/uncommitted at resume time along with several other supporting files — these changes may not yet be committed.
Spawn tabs are intentionally not restored; if PTY resurrection across restarts becomes desirable, that would require a more complex solution.
The 500ms polling loop for live sessions in SessionDetail.tsx is a workaround for the server's 2s broadcast cadence — a push-based update mechanism could replace this.
